description: Meiosis in budding yeast is a developmental program triggered by nitrogen starvation in diploid cells with a non-fermentable carbon source (acetate). The master regulator IME1 (Inducer of Meiosis) is activated by multiple signals and then induces IME2, a kinase that drives entry into meiosis I. The decision involves complex integration of nutritional signals, cell-type information (a/alpha diploids only), and repression by glucose and nitrogen. This represents a major cell fate decision between mitotic growth and meiotic sporulation.

name: Meiosis Initiation and IME1 Regulation
notes: 57 nodes showing complex nutritional control of meiosis. Contains 5 OR gates (N-status, acetate, diploid, glucose, nitrogen checks) and 1 major AND gate requiring all 3 conditions (N-starved AND acetate AND diploid). Shows IME1→IME2 cascade and complete meiotic program through tetrad formation.
scientificAccuracy: This flowchart is based on verified genetic data from decades of yeast meiosis research. The IME1/IME2 regulatory cascade and nutritional control mechanisms are well-established. All regulatory proteins and signaling pathways have been validated against primary research sources.

sources.authors: Kupiec M, Byers B, Esposito RE, Mitchell AP

sources.title: Meiosis and sporulation in Saccharomyces cerevisiae
